skill.name: stripe-automation
skill.description: Automate Stripe tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): customers, charges, subscriptions, invoices, products, refunds. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Stripe payment automation via Rube MCP - manage customers, subscriptions, invoices, products, and payments through AI.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 75 static findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' patterns are Markdown code formatting backticks in documentation, not executable code. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are false positives from API tool names containing 'create', 'search', 'list'. The hardcoded URL is the legitimate Rube MCP endpoint. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code, no security risks, and clear intent to help users automate Stripe operations through official APIs.

low_findings.title: MCP Server Dependency
low_findings.description: Requires external Rube MCP server connection. Users must verify they trust the Rube.app service before connecting, as it will act as an intermediary for Stripe API operations.

content.value_statement: Stop manually managing Stripe through dashboards. This skill enables AI agents to handle customer management, subscription billing, invoicing, and payment operations through natural language commands, reducing billing workflow time by 80%.
actual_capabilities: Create and update Stripe customers with email, name, and metadata
List and search customers, charges, subscriptions, invoices, products, and prices
Create payment intents and confirm payments through Stripe API
Manage subscriptions including creation, updates, and cancellation
Generate and manage invoices with automatic or manual collection methods
Process partial and full refunds on charges with reason tracking
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP server connection and authenticated Stripe account
Cannot execute Stripe operations without completing OAuth authentication flow first
Rate limits imposed by Stripe API may affect bulk operations
Tool schemas may change over time; requires searching tools before each operation

use_cases.title: Customer Onboarding Automation
use_cases.description: SaaS founders use natural language to create Stripe customers, set up subscriptions, and generate invoices during user signup without leaving their development environment.

use_cases.title: Billing Support Workflows
use_cases.description: Support teams query customer payment history, process refunds, update subscription plans, and generate invoice links through conversational AI commands.

use_cases.title: Financial Reconciliation
use_cases.description: Finance professionals list charges, search transactions by date range, export payment data, and audit subscriptions across multiple customers using AI queries.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new customer
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Stripe customer for john@example.com with name 'John Smith' and metadata set to {source: 'website', plan: 'premium'}
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to add a new customer to their Stripe account during signup

prompt_templates.title: Set up subscription billing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a subscription for customer cus_12345 using price_id price_abc123 with quantity 2
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to start billing a customer for a recurring subscription

prompt_templates.title: Process a refund
prompt_templates.prompt: Issue a full refund for charge ch_67890 with reason 'requested_by_customer'
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to refund a payment due to customer request or cancellation

prompt_templates.title: Generate invoice report
prompt_templates.prompt: List all invoices for customer cus_12345 from the last 30 days and show their status and amounts
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to review billing history for a specific customer

output_examples.input: Create a customer for sarah@company.com with name 'Sarah Johnson'
output_examples.output: Customer created successfully. Customer ID: cus_abc123xyz, Email: sarah@company.com, Name: Sarah Johnson, Created: 2025-02-25T10:30:00Z

output_examples.input: List all subscriptions for customer cus_12345
output_examples.output: Found 2 subscriptions for cus_12345: 1) ID: sub_abc123, Status: active, Price: price_xxx ($29/month), Current period end: 2025-03-25. 2) ID: sub_def456, Status: past_due, Price: price_yyy ($99/year)

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first before executing any Stripe operation to get current API schemas
Search for existing customers by email before creating new ones to prevent duplicate customer records
Verify Rube MCP connection status shows ACTIVE before running any Stripe workflows
Use payment intents instead of direct charges for better security and 3D Secure support
anti_patterns: Creating customers without searching first - this leads to duplicate customer records in Stripe
Using uppercase currency codes like 'USD' instead of lowercase 'usd' - this causes API validation errors
Forgetting that amounts are in cents (100 = $1.00) - entering decimal amounts like '10.50' will be interpreted as 1050 cents
Assuming subscriptions work immediately - customers must have valid payment methods attached before subscriptions can be created

faq.question: Do I need a Stripe API key?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses Rube MCP which handles Stripe authentication through OAuth. You just need to connect your Stripe account via RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS.

faq.question: What happens if my Stripe account is not connected?
faq.answer: The skill will instruct you to authenticate via RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS. You must complete the OAuth flow before any Stripe operations will work.

faq.question: Can I process real payments with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, if your Stripe connection is active and in live mode. Be cautious as operations affect your actual Stripe account and cannot be undone.

faq.question: Why do I need to search tools before every operation?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas may change. Calling RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS ensures the AI has the latest parameter names and requirements for each Stripe API call.

faq.question: How do currency amounts work?
faq.answer: Stripe uses the smallest currency unit. For USD/EUR, enter amounts in cents (10000 = $100.00). For zero-decimal currencies like JPY, enter the exact amount (1000 = ¥1000).

faq.question: Can I batch process multiple customers?
faq.answer: Yes, but Stripe has rate limits. Use pagination with limit parameters (max 100) and process in batches to avoid hitting API rate limits.
